prompt: An elevator serves floors numbered 1 through 10. It starts on floor 1 moving in the up direction. It performs 12 numbered moves. On move number k (for k = 1, 2, ..., 12) the elevator takes exactly k single-floor steps, one after another. Before each single-floor step the following rule is applied: if the elevator is currently on floor 10 its direction is set to down; if it is currently on floor 1 its direction is set to up; otherwise the direction is unchanged. It then moves one floor in the current direction. (So the elevator bounces off the top and bottom floors like a reflecting wall.) Determine which floor the elevator is on immediately after completing move number 12. Reason step by step -- tracking direction reversals carefully or using the total step count -- then give the final floor as a single integer on the last line in the form \boxed{X}.

grading._solution: Total single-floor steps = 1+2+...+12 = 78. The motion is a triangle wave reflecting in [1,10]: from floor 1 going up it takes 9 steps to reach 10, then 9 steps back down to 1, a period of 18 steps that returns to floor 1 moving up. 78 mod 18 = 6. Starting at floor 1 moving up, 6 steps up lands on floor 1+6 = 7. Direct step-by-step simulation of all 78 steps confirms the final floor is 7.
